chapter.title: Furtul de perle de la Grand Metropolitan

sentences.text: Poirot și cu mine petrecem câteva zile la Brighton.
sentences.translationRu: Мы с Пуаро проводим несколько дней в Брайтоне.
sentences.original: “Poirot,” I said, “a change of air would do you good.” “You think so, mon ami?” “I am sure of it.” “Eh—eh?” said my friend, smiling. “It is all arranged, then?” “You will come?” “Where do you propose to take me?”

sentences.text: Stăm la Grand Metropolitan,
sentences.original: “Brighton. As a matter of fact, a friend of mine in the City put me on to a very good thing, and—well, I have money to burn, as the saying goes.

sentences.text: un hotel mare și scump de lângă mare.
sentences.original: I think a week-end at the Grand Metropolitan would do us all the good in the world.”

sentences.text: Soarele strălucește și aerul este plăcut.
sentences.original: “Thank you, I accept most gratefully. You have the good heart to think of an old man. And the good heart, it is in the end worth all the little grey cells. Yes, yes, I who speak to you am in danger of forgetting that sometimes.” I did not quite relish the implication. I fancy that Poirot is sometimes a little inclined to underestimate my mental capacities. But his pleasure was so evident that I put my slight annoyance aside. “Then, that’s all right,” I said hastily.

sentences.text: Luăm prânzul în restaurantul hotelului.
sentences.original: Saturday evening saw us dining at the Grand Metropolitan in the midst of a gay throng.

sentences.text: În jurul nostru sunt mulți oameni bogați.
sentences.original: All the world and his wife seemed to be at Brighton. The dresses were marvellous, and the jewels—worn sometimes with more love of display than good taste—were something magnificent.

sentences.text: Poirot privește totul cu interes.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро смотрит на всё с интересом.
sentences.original: “Hein, it is a sight this!” murmured Poirot. “This is the home of the Profiteer, is it not so, Hastings?” “Supposed to be,” I replied. “But we’ll hope they aren’t all tarred with the Profiteering brush.” Poirot gazed round him placidly.

sentences.text: Vorbim despre bani și despre afaceri.
sentences.translationRu: Мы говорим о деньгах и о делах.
sentences.original: “The sight of so many jewels makes me wish I had turned my brains to crime, instead of to its detection. What a magnificent opportunity for some thief of distinction!

sentences.text: La masa de lângă noi stau un domn și o doamnă.
sentences.translationRu: За соседним столом сидят господин и дама.
sentences.original: Regard, Hastings, that stout woman by the pillar. She is, as you would say, plastered with gems.” I followed his eyes.

sentences.text: Sunt domnul Opalsen și soția lui.
sentences.translationRu: Это мистер Опалсен и его жена.
sentences.original: “Why,” I exclaimed, “it’s Mrs. Opalsen.” “You know her?”

sentences.text: Domnul Opalsen este un om de afaceri din Londra,
sentences.original: “Slightly. Her husband is a rich stockbroker who made a fortune in the recent Oil boom.”

sentences.text: iar soția lui poartă haine elegante.
sentences.original: After dinner we ran across the Opalsens in the lounge, and I introduced Poirot to them. We chatted for a few minutes, and ended by having our coffee together.

sentences.text: Doamna Opalsen ne vorbește prietenoasă.
sentences.translationRu: Миссис Опалсен дружелюбно заговаривает с нами.
sentences.original: Poirot said a few words in praise of some of the costlier gems displayed on the lady’s ample bosom, and she brightened up at once.

sentences.text: Curând începe să vorbească despre perlele ei.
sentences.translationRu: Вскоре она начинает рассказывать о своём жемчуге.
sentences.original: “It’s a perfect hobby of mine, Mr. Poirot. I just love jewellery. Ed knows my weakness, and every time things go well he brings me something new. You are interested in precious stones?” “I have had a good deal to do with them one time and another, madame. My profession has brought me into contact with some of the most famous jewels in the world.” He went on to narrate, with discreet pseudonyms, the story of the historic jewels of a reigning house, and Mrs. Opalsen listened with bated breath.

sentences.text: Perlele ei sunt foarte cunoscute,
sentences.original: “There now!” she exclaimed, as he ended. “If it isn’t just like a play! You know, I’ve got some pearls of my own that have a history attached to them.

sentences.text: și valorează o mulțime de bani.
sentences.original: I believe it’s supposed to be one of the finest necklaces in the world—the pearls are so beautifully matched and so perfect in colour.

sentences.text: Sunt cele mai frumoase perle din lume, spune ea mândră.
sentences.translationRu: «Это красивейший жемчуг на свете», — гордо говорит она.
sentences.original: I declare I really must run up and get it!” “Oh, madame,” protested Poirot, “you are too amiable. Pray do not derange yourself!”

sentences.text: Soțul meu mi le-a dăruit.
sentences.original: “Oh, but I’d like to show it to you.”

sentences.text: Trebuie să vi le arăt neapărat.
sentences.original: The buxom dame waddled across to the lift briskly enough.

sentences.text: Domnul Opalsen zâmbește lângă ea.
sentences.translationRu: Мистер Опалсен улыбается рядом с ней.
sentences.original: Her husband, who had been talking to me, looked at Poirot inquiringly. “Madame your wife is so amiable as to insist on showing me her pearl necklace,” explained the latter.

sentences.text: Soția mea iubește mult aceste perle, spune el.
sentences.translationRu: «Моя жена очень любит этот жемчуг», — говорит он.
sentences.original: “Oh, the pearls!” Opalsen smiled in a satisfied fashion. “Well, they are worth seeing. Cost a pretty penny too!

sentences.text: Le ține mereu în camera noastră.
sentences.translationRu: «Она всегда держит его в нашем номере».
sentences.original: Still, the money’s there all right; I could get what I paid for them any day—perhaps more. May have to, too, if things go on as they are now. Money’s confoundedly tight in the City. All this infernal E.P.D.” He rambled on, launching into technicalities where I could not follow him.

sentences.text: Doamna Opalsen se ridică de la masă.
sentences.original: He was interrupted by a small page-boy who approached and murmured something in his ear.

sentences.text: Mă duc sus să le aduc, spune ea.
sentences.translationRu: «Я поднимусь и принесу его», — говорит она.
sentences.original: “Eh—what? I’ll come at once. Not taken ill, is she? Excuse me, gentlemen.” He left us abruptly.

sentences.text: Ea urcă în camera de la etaj.
sentences.translationRu: Она поднимается в номер на этаже.
sentences.original: Poirot leaned back and lit one of his tiny Russian cigarettes. Then, carefully and meticulously, he arranged the empty coffee-cups in a neat row, and beamed happily on the result.

sentences.text: Noi rămânem jos și o așteptăm.
sentences.translationRu: Мы остаёмся внизу и ждём её.
sentences.original: The minutes passed. The Opalsens did not return. “Curious,” I remarked, at length. “I wonder when they will come back.”

sentences.text: Trec câteva minute liniștite.
sentences.original: Poirot watched the ascending spirals of smoke, and then said thoughtfully: “They will not come back.” “Why?” “Because, my friend, something has happened.” “What sort of thing? How do you know?” I asked curiously. Poirot smiled.

sentences.text: Deodată auzim un strigăt de sus.
sentences.original: “A few moments ago the manager came hurriedly out of his office and ran upstairs. He was much agitated. The lift-boy is deep in talk with one of the pages. The lift-bell has rung three times, but he heeds it not.

sentences.text: Doamna Opalsen coboară în grabă.
sentences.original: Thirdly, even the waiters are distrait; and to make a waiter distrait——” Poirot shook his head with an air of finality. “The affair must indeed be of the first magnitude. Ah, it is as I thought! Here come the police.”

sentences.text: Fața ei este albă ca varul.
sentences.translationRu: Лицо у неё белое как мел.
sentences.original: Two men had just entered the hotel—one in uniform, the other in plain clothes. They spoke to a page, and were immediately ushered upstairs.

sentences.text: Perlele mele! Perlele mele au dispărut! strigă ea.
sentences.translationRu: «Мой жемчуг! Мой жемчуг пропал!» — кричит она.
sentences.original: A few minutes later, the same boy descended and came up to where we were sitting. “Mr. Opalsen’s compliments, and would you step upstairs.” Poirot sprang nimbly to his feet. One would have said that he awaited the summons. I followed with no less alacrity.

sentences.text: Cineva mi le-a furat din cameră!
sentences.translationRu: «Кто-то украл его у меня из номера!»
sentences.original: The Opalsens’ apartments were situated on the first floor. After knocking on the door, the page-boy retired, and we answered the summons, “Come in!” A strange scene met our eyes. The room was Mrs. Opalsen’s bedroom, and in the centre of it, lying back in an arm-chair, was the lady herself, weeping violently.

sentences.text: Doamna aproape că leșină de spaimă.
sentences.translationRu: Дама едва не падает в обморок от испуга.
sentences.original: She presented an extraordinary spectacle, with the tears making great furrows in the powder with which her complexion was liberally coated.

sentences.text: Poirot se ridică imediat în picioare.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро тут же поднимается на ноги.
sentences.original: Mr. Opalsen was striding up and down angrily. The two police officials stood in the middle of the room, one with a notebook in hand.

sentences.text: Liniștiți-vă, doamnă, spune el calm.
sentences.original: An hotel chambermaid, looking frightened to death, stood by the fire-place; and on the other side of the room a Frenchwoman, obviously Mrs. Opalsen’s maid, was weeping and wringing her hands, with an intensity of grief that rivalled that of her mistress. Into this pandemonium stepped Poirot, neat and smiling.

sentences.text: Eu sunt Hercule Poirot și vă voi ajuta.
sentences.translationRu: «Я Эркюль Пуаро, и я вам помогу».
sentences.original: Immediately, with an energy surprising in one of her bulk, Mrs. Opalsen sprang from her chair towards him. “There now; Ed may say what he likes, but I believe in luck, I do. It was fated I should meet you the way I did this evening, and I’ve a feeling that if you can’t get my pearls back for me nobody can.”

sentences.text: Acum mergem cu toții în camera dumneavoastră.
sentences.translationRu: «Сейчас мы все вместе пойдём в ваш номер».
sentences.original: “Calm yourself, I pray of you, madame.” Poirot patted her hand soothingly. “Reassure yourself. All will be well. Hercule Poirot will aid you!”

sentences.text: Urcăm scările împreună cu soții Opalsen.
sentences.translationRu: Мы поднимаемся по лестнице вместе с супругами Опалсен.
sentences.original: Mr. Opalsen turned to the police inspector. “There will be no objection to my—er—calling in this gentleman, I suppose?” “None at all, sir,” replied the man civilly, but with complete indifference. “Perhaps now your lady’s feeling better she’ll just let us have the facts?” Mrs. Opalsen looked helplessly at Poirot. He led her back to her chair.

sentences.text: Intrăm în camera lor de la hotel.
sentences.translationRu: Мы входим в их гостиничный номер.
sentences.original: “Seat yourself, madame, and recount to us the whole history without agitating yourself.” Thus abjured, Mrs. Opalsen dried her eyes gingerly, and began.

sentences.text: Pe o masă mică stă o cutie de bijuterii.
sentences.translationRu: На столике стоит шкатулка для драгоценностей.
sentences.original: “I came upstairs after dinner to fetch my pearls for Mr. Poirot here to see. The chambermaid and Célestine were both in the room as usual——” “Excuse me, madame, but what do you mean by ‘as usual’?” Mr. Opalsen explained. “I make it a rule that no one is to come into this room unless Célestine, the maid, is there also. The chambermaid does the room in the morning while Célestine is present, and comes in after dinner to turn down the beds under the same conditions; otherwise she never enters the room.”

sentences.text: Cutia este deschisă și complet goală.
sentences.original: “Well, as I was saying,” continued Mrs. Opalsen, “I came up. I went to the drawer here,”—she indicated the bottom right-hand drawer of the knee-hole dressing-table—“took out my jewel-case and unlocked it. It seemed quite as usual—but the pearls were not there!”

sentences.text: Doamna ne explică unde stăteau perlele.
sentences.original: The inspector had been busy with his notebook. “When had you last seen them?” he asked.

sentences.text: Le țineam în această cutie, spune ea.
sentences.translationRu: «Я держала его в этой шкатулке», — говорит она.
sentences.original: “They were there when I went down to dinner.” “You are sure?” “Quite sure. I was uncertain whether to wear them or not, but in the end I decided on the emeralds, and put them back in the jewel-case.”

sentences.text: Iar cutia stătea într-un sertar.
sentences.original: “Who locked up the jewel-case?” “I did. I wear the key on a chain round my neck.” She held it up as she spoke. The inspector examined it, and shrugged his shoulders. “The thief must have had a duplicate key. No difficult matter. The lock is quite a simple one. What did you do after you’d locked the jewel-case?” “I put it back in the bottom drawer where I always keep it.”

sentences.text: Acum totul a dispărut.
sentences.original: “You didn’t lock the drawer?” “No, I never do. My maid remains in the room till I come up, so there’s no need.”

sentences.text: În cameră se află o femeie tânără.
sentences.original: The inspector’s face grew graver. “Am I to understand that the jewels were there when you went down to dinner, and that since then the maid has not left the room?”

sentences.text: Ea este Célestine, camerista doamnei.
sentences.original: Suddenly, as though the horror of her own situation for the first time burst upon her, Célestine uttered a piercing shriek, and, flinging herself upon Poirot, poured out a torrent of incoherent French.

sentences.text: Célestine este franțuzoaică și foarte agitată.
sentences.original: The suggestion was infamous! That she should be suspected of robbing Madame! The police were well known to be of a stupidity incredible! But Monsieur, who was a Frenchman— “A Belgian,” interjected Poirot, but Célestine paid no attention to the correction.

sentences.text: Ea plânge și tremură de frică.
sentences.translationRu: Она плачет и дрожит от страха.
sentences.original: Monsieur would not stand by and see her falsely accused, while that infamous chambermaid was allowed to go scot-free. She had never liked her—a bold, red-faced thing—a born thief.

sentences.text: Poirot îi pune câteva întrebări.
sentences.original: She had said from the first that she was not honest. And had kept a sharp watch over her too, when she was doing Madame’s room! Let those idiots of policemen search her, and if they did not find Madame’s pearls on her it would be very surprising!

sentences.text: Ai stat în cameră tot timpul? întreabă el.
sentences.translationRu: «Ты была в номере всё время?» — спрашивает он.
sentences.original: Although this harangue was uttered in rapid and virulent French, Célestine had interlarded it with a wealth of gesture, and the chambermaid realized at least a part of her meaning. She reddened angrily.

sentences.text: Da, domnule, dar am ieșit de două ori.
sentences.original: “If that foreign woman’s saying I took the pearls, it’s a lie!” she declared heatedly. “I never so much as saw them.” “Search her!” screamed the other. “You will find it is as I say.”

sentences.text: Am ieșit doar pentru câteva clipe, spune ea.
sentences.translationRu: «Я выходила лишь на несколько мгновений», — говорит она.
sentences.original: “You’re a liar—do you hear?” said the chambermaid, advancing upon her. “Stole ’em yourself, and want to put it on me. Why, I was only in the room about three minutes before the lady come up, and then you were sitting here the whole time, as you always do, like a cat watching a mouse.”

sentences.text: De ce ai ieșit din cameră? întreabă Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: «Почему ты выходила из номера?» — спрашивает Пуаро.
sentences.original: The inspector looked across inquiringly at Célestine. “Is that true? Didn’t you leave the room at all?” “I did not actually leave her alone,” admitted Célestine reluctantly, “but I went into my own room through the door here twice—once to fetch a reel of cotton, and once for my scissors. She must have done it then.”

sentences.text: Doamna mi-a cerut niște lucruri.
sentences.original: “You wasn’t gone a minute,” retorted the chambermaid angrily. “Just popped out and in again. I’d be glad if the police would search me. I’ve nothing to be afraid of.”

sentences.text: M-am dus repede și m-am întors repede.
sentences.original: At this moment there was a tap at the door. The inspector went to it. His face brightened when he saw who it was. “Ah!” he said. “That’s rather fortunate. I sent for one of our female searchers, and she’s just arrived. Perhaps if you wouldn’t mind going into the room next door.”

sentences.text: Cât ai fost plecată, a venit cineva?
sentences.original: He looked at the chambermaid, who stepped across the threshold with a toss of her head, the searcher following her closely. The French girl had sunk sobbing into a chair.

sentences.text: Da, camerista hotelului a intrat.
sentences.original: Poirot was looking round the room, the main features of which I have made clear by a sketch. [A sketch of the bedroom, adjoining maid's room, and corridor.] “Where does that door lead?” he inquired, nodding his head towards the one by the window.

sentences.text: Ea a venit să facă curat în cameră.
sentences.original: “Into the next apartment, I believe,” said the inspector. “It’s bolted, anyway, on this side.” Poirot walked across to it, tried it, then drew back the bolt and tried it again. “And on the other side as well,” he remarked. “Well, that seems to rule out that.”

sentences.text: Sunt sigură că ea a furat perlele! strigă Célestine.
sentences.translationRu: «Я уверена, что жемчуг украла она!» — кричит Селестина.
sentences.original: He walked over to the windows, examining each of them in turn. “And again—nothing. Not even a balcony outside.” “Even if there were,” said the inspector impatiently, “I don’t see how that would help us, if the maid never left the room.” “Évidemment,” said Poirot, not disconcerted. “As Mademoiselle is positive she did not leave the room——”

sentences.text: Camerista hotelului este chemată în cameră.
sentences.original: He was interrupted by the reappearance of the chambermaid and the police searcher. “Nothing,” said the latter laconically.

sentences.text: Ea este o femeie tânără și speriată.
sentences.original: “I should hope not, indeed,” said the chambermaid virtuously. “And that French hussy ought to be ashamed of herself taking away an honest girl’s character!”

sentences.text: Nu am furat nimic! spune ea.
sentences.original: “There, there, my girl; that’s all right,” said the inspector, opening the door. “Nobody suspects you. You go along and get on with your work.” The chambermaid went unwillingly.

sentences.text: Am făcut doar curat și am plecat.
sentences.original: “Going to search her?” she demanded, pointing at Célestine. “Yes, yes!” He shut the door on her and turned the key.

sentences.text: Cele două femei încep să se certe.
sentences.original: Célestine accompanied the searcher into the small room in her turn. A few minutes later she also returned. Nothing had been found on her.

sentences.text: Fiecare spune că cealaltă este hoața.
sentences.original: The inspector’s face grew graver. “I’m afraid I’ll have to ask you to come along with me all the same, miss.” He turned to Mrs. Opalsen. “I’m sorry, madam, but all the evidence points that way. If she’s not got them on her, they’re hidden somewhere about the room.”

sentences.text: Poirot le ascultă în liniște.
sentences.original: Célestine uttered a piercing shriek, and clung to Poirot’s arm. The latter bent and whispered something in the girl’s ear. She looked up at him doubtfully. “Si, si, mon enfant—I assure you it is better not to resist.”

sentences.text: El nu spune încă nimic.
sentences.original: Then he turned to the inspector. “You permit, monsieur? A little experiment—purely for my own satisfaction.” “Depends on what it is,” replied the police officer non-committally.

sentences.text: Domnul Opalsen cheamă poliția.
sentences.original: Poirot addressed Célestine once more. “You have told us that you went into your room to fetch a reel of cotton. Whereabouts was it?” “On the top of the chest of drawers, monsieur.” “And the scissors?” “They also.”

sentences.text: Poliția sosește repede la hotel.
sentences.original: “Would it be troubling you too much, mademoiselle, to ask you to repeat those two actions? You were sitting here with your work, you say?” Célestine sat down, and then, at a sign from Poirot, rose, passed into the adjoining room, took up an object from the chest of drawers, and returned.

sentences.text: Polițiștii caută în toată camera.
sentences.original: Poirot divided his attention between her movements and a large turnip of a watch which he held in the palm of his hand. “Again, if you please, mademoiselle.” At the conclusion of the second performance, he made a note in his pocket-book, and returned the watch to his pocket.

sentences.text: Se uită în fiecare colț și în fiecare sertar.
sentences.translationRu: Они заглядывают в каждый угол и в каждый ящик.
sentences.original: “Thank you, mademoiselle. And you, monsieur,”—he bowed to the inspector—“for your courtesy.” The inspector seemed somewhat entertained by this excessive politeness. Célestine departed in a flood of tears, accompanied by the woman and the plain-clothes official.

sentences.text: Poliția le caută și pe cele două femei.
sentences.original: Then, with a brief apology to Mrs. Opalsen, the inspector set to work to ransack the room.

sentences.text: Le caută hainele cu mare grijă.
sentences.original: He pulled out drawers, opened cupboards, completely unmade the bed, and tapped the floor.

sentences.text: Dar nu găsesc nicăieri perlele.
sentences.original: Mr. Opalsen looked on sceptically. “You really think you will find them?” “Yes, sir. It stands to reason. She hadn’t time to take them out of the room. The lady’s discovering the robbery so soon upset her plans.

sentences.text: Toți sunt nedumeriți.
sentences.original: No, they’re here right enough. One of the two must have hidden them—and it’s very unlikely for the chambermaid to have done so.”

sentences.text: În tot acest timp, Poirot examinează camera.
sentences.translationRu: Всё это время Пуаро осматривает номер.
sentences.original: “More than unlikely—impossible!” said Poirot quietly. “Eh?” The inspector stared. Poirot smiled modestly.

sentences.text: Se apleacă și se uită la sertar.
sentences.original: “I will demonstrate. Hastings, my good friend, take my watch in your hand—with care. It is a family heirloom! Just now I timed Mademoiselle’s movements—her first absence from the room was of twelve seconds, her second of fifteen. Now observe my actions.

sentences.text: Atinge marginile sertarului cu degetul.
sentences.original: Madame will have the kindness to give me the key of the jewel-case. I thank you. My friend Hastings will have the kindness to say ‘Go!’” “Go!” I said.

sentences.text: Sertarul este uns cu puțină grăsime.
sentences.original: With almost incredible swiftness, Poirot wrenched open the drawer of the dressing-table, extracted the jewel-case, fitted the key in the lock, opened the case, selected a piece of jewellery, shut and locked the case, and returned it to the drawer, which he pushed to again. His movements were like lightning.

sentences.text: Poirot ridică din sprâncene, mirat.
sentences.original: “Well, mon ami?” he demanded of me breathlessly. “Forty-six seconds,” I replied. “You see?” He looked round. “There would not have been time for the chambermaid even to take the necklace out, far less hide it.”

sentences.text: Interesant, foarte interesant, murmură el.
sentences.original: “Then that settles it on the maid,” said the inspector with satisfaction, and returned to his search. He passed into the maid’s bedroom next door.

sentences.text: Eu îl întreb ce a găsit.
sentences.translationRu: Я спрашиваю его, что он нашёл.
sentences.original: Poirot was frowning thoughtfully. Suddenly he shot a question at Mr. Opalsen. “This necklace—it was, without doubt, insured?” Mr. Opalsen looked a trifle surprised at the question. “Yes,” he said hesitatingly, “that is so.”

sentences.text: Vei vedea în curând, prietene, răspunde el.
sentences.original: “But what does that matter?” broke in Mrs. Opalsen tearfully. “It’s my necklace I want. It was unique. No money could be the same.”

sentences.text: Poirot iese pe coridor.
sentences.original: “I comprehend, madame,” said Poirot soothingly. “I comprehend perfectly. To la femme sentiment is everything—is it not so? But monsieur, who has not the so fine susceptibility, will doubtless find some slight consolation in the fact.” “Of course, of course,” said Mr. Opalsen rather uncertainly. “Still——”

sentences.text: Lângă camera doamnei este o altă cameră.
sentences.translationRu: Рядом с номером дамы есть другой номер.
sentences.original: He was interrupted by a shout of triumph from the inspector. He came in dangling something from his fingers.

sentences.text: Acea cameră este goală în acel moment.
sentences.translationRu: Тот номер в это время пуст.
sentences.original: With a cry, Mrs. Opalsen heaved herself up from her chair. She was a changed woman. “Oh, oh, my necklace!” She clasped it to her breast with both hands. We crowded round.

sentences.text: Între cele două camere există o ușă.
sentences.translationRu: Между этими двумя номерами есть дверь.
sentences.original: “Where was it?” demanded Opalsen. “Maid’s bed. In among the springs of the wire mattress.

sentences.text: Poirot deschide ușa dintre camere.
sentences.original: She must have stolen it and hidden it there before the chambermaid arrived on the scene.”

sentences.text: Ușa se deschide fără niciun zgomot.
sentences.original: “You permit, madame?” said Poirot gently. He took the necklace from her and examined it closely; then handed it back with a bow.

sentences.text: El zâmbește mulțumit pentru sine.
sentences.original: “I’m afraid, madam, you’ll have to hand it over to us for the time being,” said the inspector. “We shall want it for the charge. But it shall be returned to you as soon as possible.” Mr. Opalsen frowned. “Is that necessary?” “I’m afraid so, sir. Just a formality.”

sentences.text: Acum încep să înțeleg, spune el încet.
sentences.translationRu: «Теперь я начинаю понимать», — тихо говорит он.
sentences.original: “Oh, let him take it, Ed!” cried his wife. “I’d feel safer if he did. I shouldn’t sleep a wink thinking some one else might try and get hold of it. That wretched girl! And I would never have believed it of her.” “There, there, my dear, don’t take on so.”

sentences.text: Deodată, un polițist strigă din camera doamnei.
sentences.translationRu: Вдруг полицейский кричит из номера дамы.
sentences.original: I felt a gentle pressure on my arm. It was Poirot. “Shall we slip away, my friend? I think our services are no longer needed.”

sentences.text: Am găsit ceva! spune el.
sentences.original: Once outside, however, he hesitated, and then, much to my surprise, he remarked: “I should rather like to see the room next door.” The door was not locked, and we entered.

sentences.text: În patul cameristei hotelului sunt perle.
sentences.translationRu: В постели горничной отеля лежит жемчуг.
sentences.original: The room, which was a large double one, was unoccupied. Dust lay about rather noticeably, and my sensitive friend gave a characteristic grimace as he ran his finger round a rectangular mark on a table near the window. “The service leaves to be desired,” he observed dryly.

sentences.text: Perlele erau ascunse sub saltea.
sentences.original: He was staring thoughtfully out of the window, and seemed to have fallen into a brown study. “Well?” I demanded impatiently. “What did we come in here for?” He started. “Je vous demande pardon, mon ami. I wished to see if the door was really bolted on this side also.”

sentences.text: Toți se uită la camerista hotelului.
sentences.original: “Well,” I said, glancing at the door which communicated with the room we had just left, “it is bolted.” Poirot nodded. He still seemed to be thinking.

sentences.text: Acum toți cred că ea este hoața.
sentences.translationRu: Теперь все думают, что воровка — она.
sentences.original: “And, anyway,” I continued, “what does it matter? The case is over. I wish you’d had more chance of distinguishing yourself. But it was the kind of case that even a stiff-backed idiot like that inspector couldn’t go wrong over.”

sentences.text: Camerista plânge cu lacrimi amare.
sentences.original: Poirot shook his head. “The case is not over, my friend. It will not be over until we find out who stole the pearls.”

sentences.text: Nu eu am pus perlele acolo, jur! spune ea.
sentences.translationRu: «Это не я положила туда жемчуг, клянусь!» — говорит она.
sentences.original: “But the maid did!” “Why do you say that?” “Why,” I stammered, “they were found—actually in her mattress.”

sentences.text: Célestine pare ușurată și triumfătoare.
sentences.original: “Ta, ta, ta!” said Poirot impatiently. “Those were not the pearls.” “What?”

sentences.text: Vedeți? Ea este hoața, nu eu! spune ea.
sentences.translationRu: «Видите? Воровка она, а не я!» — говорит она.
sentences.original: “Imitation, mon ami.” The statement took my breath away. Poirot was smiling placidly.

sentences.text: Dar Poirot ridică o mână și cere liniște.
sentences.translationRu: Но Пуаро поднимает руку и просит тишины.
sentences.original: “The good inspector obviously knows nothing of jewels. But presently there will be a fine hullabaloo!”

sentences.text: Poirot ia perlele găsite și le examinează.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро берёт найденный жемчуг и осматривает его.
sentences.original: “Come!” I cried, dragging at his arm. “Where?” “We must tell the Opalsens at once.” “I think not.”

sentences.text: Le ține în lumină și le privește atent.
sentences.translationRu: Он держит его на свету и внимательно разглядывает.
sentences.original: “But that poor woman——” “Eh bien; that poor woman, as you call her, will have a much better night believing the jewels to be safe.”

sentences.text: Le atinge cu degetele și chiar le încearcă cu dintele.
sentences.translationRu: Он трогает его пальцами и даже пробует на зуб.
sentences.original: “But the thief may escape with them!” “As usual, my friend, you speak without reflection. How do you know that the pearls Mrs. Opalsen locked up so carefully to-night were not the false ones, and that the real robbery did not take place at a much earlier date?” “Oh!” I said, bewildered.

sentences.text: Apoi spune ceva neașteptat.
sentences.original: “Exactly,” said Poirot, beaming. “We start again.”

sentences.text: Aceste perle nu sunt adevărate, spune el.
sentences.original: He led the way out of the room, paused a moment as though considering, and then walked down to the end of the corridor, stopping outside the small den where the chambermaids and valets of the respective floors congregated.

sentences.text: Sunt false, făcute din sticlă.
sentences.original: Our particular chambermaid appeared to be holding a small court there, and to be retailing her late experiences to an appreciative audience. She stopped in the middle of a sentence. Poirot bowed with his usual politeness.

sentences.text: Toți din cameră rămân uimiți.
sentences.original: “Excuse that I derange you, but I shall be obliged if you will unlock for me the door of Mr. Opalsen’s room.” The woman rose willingly, and we accompanied her down the passage again.

sentences.text: False? întreabă doamna Opalsen, palidă.
sentences.original: Mr. Opalsen’s room was on the other side of the corridor, its door facing that of his wife’s room. The chambermaid unlocked it with her pass-key, and we entered.

sentences.text: Da, doamnă, acestea nu sunt perlele dumneavoastră.
sentences.original: As she was about to depart Poirot detained her. “One moment; have you ever seen among the effects of Mr. Opalsen a card like this?” He held out a plain white card, rather highly glazed and uncommon in appearance.

sentences.text: Cineva le-a pus aici cu un scop, spune Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: «Кто-то нарочно их сюда подложил», — говорит Пуаро.
sentences.original: The maid took it and scrutinized it carefully. “No, sir, I can’t say I have. But, anyway, the valet has most to do with the gentlemen’s rooms.” “I see. Thank you.”

sentences.text: Perlele false trebuiau să pară furate.
sentences.translationRu: Поддельный жемчуг должен был сойти за украденный.
sentences.original: Poirot took back the card. The woman departed. Poirot appeared to reflect a little. Then he gave a short, sharp nod of the head.

sentences.text: Așa, vina cădea pe camerista hotelului.
sentences.translationRu: Так вина легла бы на горничную отеля.
sentences.original: “Ring the bell, I pray of you, Hastings. Three times, for the valet.” I obeyed, devoured with curiosity. Meanwhile Poirot had emptied the waste-paper-basket on the floor, and was swiftly going through its contents.

sentences.text: Camerista nu este hoața, spune Poirot ferm.
sentences.original: In a few moments the valet answered the bell. To him Poirot put the same question, and handed him the card to examine. But the response was the same. The valet had never seen a card of that particular quality among Mr. Opalsen’s belongings.

sentences.text: Cineva a vrut să o învinovățească pe nedrept.
sentences.original: Poirot thanked him, and he withdrew, somewhat unwillingly, with an inquisitive glance at the overturned waste-paper-basket and the litter on the floor. He could hardly have helped overhearing Poirot’s thoughtful remark as he bundled the torn papers back again: “And the necklace was heavily insured. . . .”

sentences.text: Perlele adevărate sunt deja departe de aici.
sentences.original: “Poirot,” I cried, “I see——” “You see nothing, my friend,” he replied quickly. “As usual, nothing at all! It is incredible—but there it is. Let us return to our own apartments.”

sentences.text: Acum Poirot le explică tuturor adevărul.
sentences.original: We did so in silence. Once there, to my intense surprise, Poirot effected a rapid change of clothing. “I go to London to-night,” he explained. “It is imperative.” “What?”

sentences.text: Furtul a fost făcut de doi oameni împreună, spune el.
sentences.original: “Absolutely. The real work, that of the brain (ah, those brave little grey cells), it is done. I go to seek the confirmation. I shall find it! Impossible to deceive Hercule Poirot!” “You’ll come a cropper one of these days,” I observed, rather disgusted by his vanity.

sentences.text: Unul este camerista hotelului.
sentences.original: “Do not be enraged, I beg of you, mon ami. I count on you to do me a service—of your friendship.” “Of course,” I said eagerly, rather ashamed of my moroseness. “What is it?”

sentences.text: Celălalt este un valet care lucrează aici.
sentences.original: “The sleeve of my coat that I have taken off—will you brush it? See you, a little white powder has clung to it. You without doubt observed me run my finger round the drawer of the dressing-table?” “No, I didn’t.”

sentences.text: Cei doi sunt hoți de bijuterii cu experiență.
sentences.original: “You should observe my actions, my friend. Thus I obtained the powder on my finger, and, being a little over-excited, I rubbed it on my sleeve; an action without method which I deplore—false to all my principles.”

sentences.text: Au plănuit totul cu mare grijă.
sentences.original: “But what was the powder?” I asked, not particularly interested in Poirot’s principles. “Not the poison of the Borgias,” replied Poirot, with a twinkle. “I see your imagination mounting. I should say it was French chalk.” “French chalk?”

sentences.text: Au așteptat momentul potrivit.
sentences.original: “Yes, cabinet-makers use it to make drawers run smoothly.” I laughed. “You old sinner! I thought you were working up to something exciting.” “Au revoir, my friend. I save myself. I fly!”

sentences.text: Iată cum au furat perlele, spune Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: «Вот как они украли жемчуг», — говорит Пуаро.
sentences.original: The door shut behind him. With a smile, half of derision, half of affection, I picked up the coat, and stretched out my hand for the clothes-brush.

sentences.text: Camerista a intrat să facă curat.
sentences.original: • • • • • • • The next morning, hearing nothing from Poirot, I went out for a stroll, met some old friends, and lunched with them at their hotel.

sentences.text: Ea a scos cutia din sertar.
sentences.original: In the afternoon we went for a spin. A punctured tyre delayed us, and it was past eight when I got back to the Grand Metropolitan.

sentences.text: De aceea sertarul era uns cu grăsime.
sentences.original: The first sight that met my eyes was Poirot, looking even more diminutive than usual, sandwiched between the Opalsens, beaming in a state of placid satisfaction.

sentences.text: Sertarul uns se deschidea fără zgomot.
sentences.original: “Mon ami Hastings!” he cried, and sprang to meet me. “Embrace me, my friend; all has marched to a marvel!” Luckily, the embrace was merely figurative—not a thing one is always sure of with Poirot. “Do you mean——” I began.

sentences.text: Nimeni de afară nu putea auzi nimic.
sentences.translationRu: Снаружи никто ничего не мог услышать.
sentences.original: “Just wonderful, I call it!” said Mrs. Opalsen, smiling all over her fat face. “Didn’t I tell you, Ed, that if he couldn’t get back my pearls nobody would?” “You did, my dear, you did. And you were right.”

sentences.text: Apoi camerista a trecut prin ușa secretă.
sentences.translationRu: Потом горничная прошла через потайную дверь.
sentences.original: I looked helplessly at Poirot, and he answered the glance. “My friend Hastings is, as you say in England, all at the seaside. Seat yourself, and I will recount to you all the affair that has so happily ended.” “Ended?” “But yes. They are arrested.” “Who are arrested?”

sentences.text: A dat cutia valetului din camera goală.
sentences.translationRu: Она передала шкатулку камердинеру в пустом номере.
sentences.original: “The chambermaid and the valet, parbleu! You did not suspect? Not with my parting hint about the French chalk?” “You said cabinet-makers used it.”

sentences.text: Valetul aștepta deja în camera de alături.
sentences.translationRu: Камердинер уже ждал в соседнем номере.
sentences.original: “Certainly they do—to make drawers slide easily. Somebody wanted that drawer to slide in and out without any noise. Who could that be? Obviously, only the chambermaid.

sentences.text: El a deschis repede cutia de bijuterii.
sentences.translationRu: Он быстро открыл шкатулку с драгоценностями.
sentences.original: The plan was so ingenious that it did not at once leap to the eye—not even to the eye of Hercule Poirot.

sentences.text: A scos perlele adevărate din ea.
sentences.translationRu: Он вынул из неё настоящий жемчуг.
sentences.original: “Listen, this was how it was done. The valet was in the empty room next door, waiting.

sentences.text: Apoi a închis cutia goală la loc.
sentences.translationRu: Потом он снова закрыл пустую шкатулку.
sentences.original: The French maid leaves the room.

sentences.text: Camerista a luat cutia goală înapoi.
sentences.original: Quick as a flash the chambermaid whips open the drawer, takes out the jewel-case, and, slipping back the bolt, passes it through the door.

sentences.text: A pus-o din nou în sertar.
sentences.translationRu: Она снова положила её в ящик.
sentences.original: The valet opens it at his leisure with the duplicate key with which he has provided himself, extracts the necklace, and waits his time.

sentences.text: Totul a durat doar câteva minute.
sentences.original: Célestine leaves the room again, and—pst!—in a flash the case is passed back again and replaced in the drawer.

sentences.text: Când doamna a urcat, perlele dispăruseră.
sentences.translationRu: Когда дама поднялась, жемчуг уже исчез.
sentences.original: “Madame arrives, the theft is discovered.

sentences.text: Perlele false au fost partea cea mai isteață, spune Poirot.
sentences.translationRu: «Поддельный жемчуг был самой хитрой частью», — говорит Пуаро.
sentences.original: The chambermaid demands to be searched, with a good deal of righteous indignation, and leaves the room without a stain on her character.

sentences.text: Hoții le-au ascuns în patul cameristei.
sentences.translationRu: Воры спрятали его в постели горничной.
sentences.original: The imitation necklace with which they have provided themselves has been concealed in the French girl’s bed that morning by the chambermaid—a master stroke, ça!”

sentences.text: Voiau ca poliția să le găsească acolo.
sentences.translationRu: Они хотели, чтобы полиция нашла его там.
sentences.original: “But what did you go to London for?” “You remember the card?” “Certainly. It puzzled me—and puzzles me still. I thought——” I hesitated delicately, glancing at Mr. Opalsen.

sentences.text: Astfel, vina cădea pe altcineva.
sentences.translationRu: Так вина легла бы на другого.
sentences.original: Poirot laughed heartily. “Une blague! For the benefit of the valet.

sentences.text: Dacă poliția găsea perlele false, ancheta se oprea.
sentences.translationRu: Если бы полиция нашла поддельный жемчуг, расследование бы прекратилось.
sentences.original: The card was one with a specially prepared surface—for finger-prints.

sentences.text: Nimeni nu ar mai fi căutat perlele adevărate.
sentences.translationRu: Настоящий жемчуг больше никто бы не искал.
sentences.original: I went straight to Scotland Yard, asked for our old friend Inspector Japp, and laid the facts before him.

sentences.text: Iar adevărații hoți ar fi scăpat liberi.
sentences.translationRu: А настоящие воры ушли бы на свободу.
sentences.original: As I had suspected, the finger-prints proved to be those of two well-known jewel thieves who have been ‘wanted’ for some time.

sentences.text: Dar Poirot a observat că perlele erau false.
sentences.translationRu: Но Пуаро заметил, что жемчуг поддельный.
sentences.original: Japp came down with me, the thieves were arrested,

sentences.text: Un hoț adevărat nu lasă perle de valoare, spune el.
sentences.translationRu: «Настоящий вор не оставляет ценный жемчуг», — говорит он.
sentences.original: and the necklace was discovered in the valet’s possession.

sentences.text: Deci aceste perle au fost lăsate intenționat.
sentences.original: A clever pair, but they failed in method.

sentences.text: Acesta a fost un truc pentru a păcăli poliția.
sentences.translationRu: Это была уловка, чтобы обмануть полицию.
sentences.original: Have I not told you, Hastings, at least thirty-six times, that without method——”

sentences.text: Acum Poirot are un plan inteligent.
sentences.translationRu: Теперь у Пуаро есть умный план.
sentences.original: “At least thirty-six thousand times!” I interrupted.

sentences.text: El cheamă camerista și valetul.
sentences.original: “But where did their ‘method’ break down?”

sentences.text: Le dă fiecăruia o carte albă în mână.
sentences.translationRu: Он даёт каждому в руки белую карточку.
sentences.original: “Mon ami, it is a good plan to take a place as chambermaid or valet—but you must not shirk your work.

sentences.text: Ei ating cartea fără să bănuiască nimic.
sentences.translationRu: Они трогают карточку, ничего не подозревая.
sentences.original: They left an empty room undusted; and therefore, when the man put down the jewel-case on the little table near the communicating door, it left a square mark——”

sentences.text: Pe carte rămân amprentele degetelor lor.
sentences.translationRu: На карточке остаются отпечатки их пальцев.
sentences.original: “I remember,” I cried.

sentences.text: Poirot ia cartea cu grijă.
sentences.original: “Before, I was undecided.

sentences.text: Acum am ce îmi trebuie, spune el zâmbind.
sentences.translationRu: «Теперь у меня есть всё нужное», — улыбаясь, говорит он.
sentences.original: Then—I knew!”

sentences.text: Poirot trimite amprentele la poliția din Londra.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро отправляет отпечатки в лондонскую полицию.
sentences.original: There was a moment’s silence.

sentences.text: Prietenul lui, inspectorul Japp, le primește.
sentences.translationRu: Их получает его друг, инспектор Джепп.
sentences.original: “And I’ve got my pearls,” said Mrs. Opalsen as a sort of Greek chorus.

sentences.text: Japp cunoaște foarte bine acești hoți.
sentences.original: “Well,” I said, “I’d better have some dinner.”

sentences.text: Ei au mai furat bijuterii și în alte hoteluri.
sentences.translationRu: Они крали драгоценности и в других гостиницах.
sentences.original: Poirot accompanied me.

sentences.text: Poliția îi arestează imediat pe cei doi hoți.
sentences.translationRu: Полиция тут же арестовывает обоих воров.
sentences.original: “This ought to mean kudos for you,” I observed.

sentences.text: Asupra lor sunt găsite perlele adevărate.
sentences.original: “Pas du tout,” replied Poirot tranquilly.

sentences.text: Perlele nu părăsiseră încă hotelul.
sentences.original: “Japp and the local inspector will divide the credit between them.

sentences.text: Doamna Opalsen își primește perlele înapoi.
sentences.translationRu: Миссис Опалсен получает свой жемчуг назад.
sentences.original: But”—he tapped his pocket—“I have a cheque here, from Mr. Opalsen,

sentences.text: Célestine este nevinovată, la fel ca cealaltă femeie.
sentences.translationRu: Селестина невиновна, как и другая женщина.
sentences.original: and, how say you, my friend?

sentences.text: Camerista hotelului care a plâns era nevinovată.
sentences.translationRu: Горничная отеля, которая плакала, была невиновна.
sentences.original: This week-end has not gone according to plan.

sentences.text: Vinovați au fost cealaltă cameristă și valetul.
sentences.translationRu: Виновны были другая горничная и камердинер.
sentences.original: Shall

sentences.text: Soții Opalsen îi mulțumesc cu căldură lui Poirot.
sentences.original: we

sentences.text: Domnul Opalsen îi oferă o recompensă mare.
sentences.translationRu: Мистер Опалсен предлагает ему большую награду.
sentences.original: return

sentences.text: Poirot acceptă cu o plecăciune elegantă.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро принимает её с изящным поклоном.
sentences.original: here

sentences.text: Mai târziu îl întreb cum a ghicit totul.
sentences.translationRu: Позже я спрашиваю его, как он всё разгадал.
sentences.original: next

sentences.text: Două lucruri mici mi-au spus adevărul, spune el.
sentences.translationRu: «Две мелочи сказали мне правду», — говорит он.
sentences.original: week-end—at

sentences.text: Întâi, grăsimea de pe sertar.
sentences.original: my

sentences.text: Apoi, perlele false din pat.
sentences.original: expense

sentences.text: Detaliile mici sunt cele mai importante, prietene.
sentences.original: this

sentences.text: Un om trebuie să folosească celulele cenușii.
sentences.original: time?”

sentences.text: Poirot zâmbește mulțumit și își netezește mustața.
sentences.translationRu: Пуаро довольно улыбается и приглаживает усы.
sentences.original: VIII
